spec_match.rationale: Correctly checks the presence of hasStorageAccess, requestStorageAccess, and the deprecated requestStorageAccessFor.

interactive.rationale: Provides 3 action buttons that execute the real API methods in the current browser, outputting detailed JSON status descriptions or runtime errors in color-coded boxes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly aligned with the parent description: checks hasStorageAccess, requestStorageAccess, and requestStorageAccessFor concurrently.

edge_cases.rationale: Implements try-catch and method-existence guards to ensure the script executes gracefully even in browsers where requestStorageAccessFor is completely stripped.

references.rationale: The page contains absolutely no see-also, reference, or external link list. It fails the repository-wide hard rule: 'Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'.

design_system.rationale: Conforms beautifully to design styling: uses standard spacing, clear comparison grids, crumb lists, and standard tables.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory ChromeStatus entry link and standard see-also footer
openQuestions.detail: To comply with the repository-wide hard rule ('Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'), we must add a 'see also' footer section containing a link to ChromeStatus entry 5162221567082496, alongside references to the PrivacyCG Storage Access specification.

summary: A highly compliant, interactive diagnostic page that runs real-time diagnostics on all Storage Access API surfaces. However, it currently fails the repository's hard rule by omitting a direct link to the ChromeStatus feature entry. Adding a standard see-also footer containing this link is a high-priority task.
